questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, the Total Expenditure (Budget Estimate) for FY 2026-27 is:
questions.explanation: Correct: ₹53.5 lakh crore. Coming to 2026-27, the non-debt receipts and the total expenditure are estimated as ₹36.5 lakh crore and ₹53.5 lakh crore respectively.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, the Centre's net tax receipts (BE 2026-27) are:
questions.explanation: Correct: ₹28.7 lakh crore. The Centre's net tax receipts are estimated at ₹28.7 lakh crore.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, The gross market borrowings in 2026-27 are estimated at:
questions.explanation: Correct: ₹17.2 lakh crore. To finance the fiscal deficit, the net market borrowings from dated securities are estimated at ₹11.7 lakh crore. The balance financing is expected to come from small savings and other sources.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, the Total Expenditure (Revised Estimate) for FY 2025-26 is:
questions.explanation: Correct: ₹49.6 lakh crore. (Revised Estimate) for FY 2025-26: The Revised Estimates of the non-debt receipts are ₹34 lakh crore of which the Centre's net tax receipts are ₹26.7 lakh crore.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, the fiscal deficit (RE 2025-26) as % of GDP is:
questions.explanation: Correct: 4.4%. In RE 2025-26, the fiscal deficit has been estimated at par with BE of 2025-26 at 4.4 percent of GDP

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, the fiscal deficit (BE 2026-27) is estimated at:
questions.explanation: Correct: 4.3%. In line with the new fiscal prudence path of debt consolidation, the fiscal deficit in BE 2026-27 is estimated to be 4.3 percent of GDP.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, the debt-to-GDP ratio is projected 50 ±1% by:
questions.explanation: Correct: 2030-31. As announced in budget speech 2025-26, in Budget 2025-26, the Central Government would target reaching a debt-to GDP ratio of 50±1 percent by 2030-31.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, the capital expenditure in 2026-27 is _____:
questions.explanation: Correct: ₹12.2 lakh crore. Capital expenditure for BE 2026-27 is estimated at ₹12.2 lakh crore.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, In the Viksit Bharat Young Leaders Dialogue 2026, several innovative ideas were shared with our Prime Minister, which have inspired many of the proposals, making this a unique _____-driven Budget:
questions.explanation: Correct: Yuva Shakti. In the Viksit Bharat Young Leaders Dialogue 2026, several innovative ideas were shared with our Prime Minister, which have inspired many of the proposals, making this a unique Yuva Shakti-driven Budget.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, how many Kartavya guide the Budget?
questions.explanation: Correct: Three. The three Kartavya are: Our first kartavya is to accelerate and sustain economic growth, by enhancing productivity and competitiveness, and building resilience to volatile global dynamics.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, which of the following is NOT one of the six intervention areas?

options.a: Scaling up manufacturing in 7 strategic and frontier sectors
options.c: Delivering a powerful push to Infrastructure

questions.explanation: Correct: Minimum Support Price reforms. The six intervention areas include manufacturing, legacy industries, Champion MSMEs, infrastructure, energy security and City Economic Regions.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the 16th Finance Commission is CORRECT?

options.a: It recommended reducing the vertical share of devolution to 38%
options.b: It submitted its report on 1st February 2026
options.c: The government has accepted the recommendation to retain vertical share at 41%
options.d: It recommended increasing the vertical share of devolution to 45%

questions.explanation: Correct: The government has accepted the recommendation to retain vertical share at 41%. The 16th Finance Commission submitted its report on 17th November 2025 and recommended retaining the vertical share of devolution at 41%, which the government has accepted

questions.question: Biopharma SHAKTI has an outlay of ₹___ crores over 5 years.
questions.explanation: Correct: 10,000. Biopharma SHAKTI has an outlay of ₹10,000 crores over the next 5 years The Strategy will include a Biopharma-focused network with 3 new National Institutes of Pharmaceutical Education and Research (NIPER) and upgrading 7 existing ones.

questions.question: What will ISM 2.0 focus on, building upon ISM 1.0?

options.c: Producing equipment and materials, designing full-stack Indian IP, and fortifying supply chains

questions.explanation: Correct: Producing equipment and materials, designing full-stack Indian IP, and fortifying supply chains. India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) 1.0 expanded India's semiconductor sector capabilities.

questions.question: The Electronics Components Manufacturing Scheme outlay is proposed to be increased from ₹22,919 crore to:
questions.explanation: Correct: ₹40,000 crore. The scheme launched in April 2025 with ₹22,919 crore will be increased to ₹40,000 crore (Paragraph 18).

questions.question: Which states are proposed to establish dedicated Rare Earth Corridors?

options.b: Odisha, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu

questions.explanation: Correct: Odisha, Kerala, Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: The Scheme for Container Manufacturing has a budgetary allocation of ₹___ crore over 5 years.
questions.explanation: Correct: 10,000. The Scheme for Container Manufacturing has an allocation of ₹10,000 crore over 5 years

questions.question: Which is NOT a sub-part of the proposed Integrated Programme for the Textile Sector?
questions.explanation: Correct: Textile Export Subsidy Scheme.

questions.question: How many legacy industrial clusters are proposed to be revived?
questions.explanation: Correct: 200. A Scheme is proposed to revive 200 legacy industrial clusters (Paragraph 26).

questions.question: What is the proposed size of the SME Growth Fund?
questions.explanation: Correct: ₹10,000 crore. A dedicated ₹10,000 crore SME Growth Fund is proposed (Paragraph 28).

questions.question: The Self-Reliant India Fund set up in 2021 is proposed to be topped up with:
questions.explanation: Correct: ₹2,000 crore. The Self-Reliant India Fund will be topped up with ₹2,000 crore (Paragraph 29).

questions.question: The first National Waterway to be operationalized under the new plan is:

options.a: Establish new Dedicated Freight Corridors connecting Dankuni in the East, to Surat in the West;
options.b: operationalise 20 new National Waterways (NW) over next 5 years, starting with NW-5 in Odisha to connect mineral rich areas of Talcher and Angul and industrial centres like Kalinga Nagar to the Ports of Paradeep and Dhamra. Training Institutes will be set up as Regional Centres of Excellence for development of the required manpower. This will benefit youth in the entire stretch of the waterways to train and acquire skills. Further, a ship repair ecosystem catering to inland waterways will also be set up at Varanasi and Patna;
options.c: launch a Coastal Cargo Promotion Scheme for incentivising a modal shift from rail and road, to increase the share of inland waterways and coastal shipping from 6 % to 12 % by 2047.

questions.explanation: Correct: NW-5 (Odisha). Training Institutes will be set up as Regional Centres of Excellence for development of the required manpower.

questions.question: What is the target for increasing the share of inland waterways and coastal shipping by 2047?
questions.explanation: Correct: From 6% to 12%. The target is to increase the share from 6% to 12% by 2047

questions.question: Ship repair ecosystems for inland waterways will be set up at:
questions.explanation: Correct: Varanasi and Patna. These will cater to inland waterways.

questions.question: What is the outlay proposed for Carbon Capture Utilization and Storage (CCUS) technologies?
questions.explanation: Correct: ₹20,000 crore over 5 years. An outlay of ₹20,000 crore is proposed over the next 5 years for CCUS

questions.question: Which five industrial sectors are specifically mentioned for CCUS applications?

options.a: Power, steel, cement, refineries and chemicals

questions.explanation: Correct: Power, steel, cement, refineries and chemicals. These five industrial sectors are mentioned for CCUS applications

questions.question: What is the proposed allocation per City Economic Region (CER) over 5 years?
questions.explanation: Correct: ₹5,000 crore. An allocation of ₹5000 crore per CER over 5 years is proposed

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a proposed High-Speed Rail corridor mentioned as 'growth connectors'?
questions.explanation: Correct: Chennai-Kolkata. The seven corridors are: i) Mumbai-Pune, ii) Pune-Hyderabad, iii) Hyderabad-Bengaluru, iv) Hyderabad-Chennai, v) Chennai-Bengaluru, vi) Delhi-Varanasi, vii) Varanasi-Siliguri (Paragraph 40).

questions.question: What is proposed to be set up to provide partial credit guarantees for infrastructure risks?
questions.explanation: Correct: Infrastructure Risk Guarantee Fund.

questions.question: What percentage of villages have banking coverage exceeding?
questions.explanation: Correct: 98%. Coverage exceeds 98% of villages in the country

questions.question: Which committee is proposed to align the banking sector with India's next phase of growth?

options.a: Committee on Banking Sector Reforms for Viksit Bharat
options.b: High Level Committee on Banking for Viksit Bharat
options.c: Banking Modernization and Future Growth Committee

questions.explanation: Correct: High Level Committee on Banking for Viksit Bharat.

questions.question: Which Public Sectors are proposed to be restructured as a first step?
questions.explanation: Correct: PFC and REC. The vision for NBFCs for Viksit Bharat has been outlined with clear targets for credit disbursement and technology adoption.

questions.question: What is the proposed investment limit for an individual PROI under the Portfolio Investment Scheme?
questions.explanation: Correct: Increased from 5% to 10%. Individual Persons Resident Outside India (PROI) will be permitted to invest in equity instruments of listed Indian companies through the Portfolio Investment Scheme.

questions.question: What incentive is proposed for municipal bond issuance by large cities?
questions.explanation: Correct: ₹100 crore for issuance over ₹1000 crore.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT proposed as a measure to leverage TReDS potential?

options.a: Mandate TReDS as transaction settlement platform for CPSE purchases from MSMEs
options.b: Introduce credit guarantee support through CGTMSE for invoice discounting
options.c: Link GeM with TReDS for sharing government purchase information
options.d: Make TReDS mandatory for all MSME transactions above ₹10 lakh

questions.explanation: Correct: Make TReDS mandatory for all MSME transactions above ₹10 lakh.

questions.question: What are 'Corporate Mitras' intended to help with?

options.b: Help MSMEs meet compliance requirements at affordable costs

questions.explanation: Correct: Help MSMEs meet compliance requirements at affordable costs. These accredited para-professionals will help MSMEs meet compliance requirements at affordable costs.

questions.question: Which institutions will design courses for 'Corporate Mitras'?
questions.explanation: Correct: ICAI, ICSI, ICMAI. Professional Institutions such as ICAI, ICSI, ICMAI will design short-term modular courses

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, which of the following groups is explicitly mentioned as a beneficiary of technology adoption?

options.d: Farmers, women in STEM, youth and Divyangjan

questions.explanation: Correct: Farmers, women in STEM, youth and Divyangjan. 21st Century is technology driven. Adoption of technology is for the benefit of all people - farmers in the field, women in STEM, youth keen to upskill and Divyangjan to access newer opportunities.

questions.question: What global share in services does India aim to achieve by 2047?
questions.explanation: Correct: 10%. The target is to make India a global leader in services, with a 10% global share by 2047

questions.question: How many Allied Health Professionals are planned to be added in 5 years?
questions.explanation: Correct: 100,000. Existing institutions for Allied Health Professionals (AHPs) will be upgraded and new AHP Institutions established in private and Government sectors.

questions.question: How many caregivers are proposed to be trained in the coming year?
questions.explanation: Correct: 150,000. In the coming year, 1.5 lakh (150,000) caregivers will be trained A strong Care Ecosystem, covering geriatric and allied care services will be built.

questions.question: How many Regional Medical Hubs are proposed to be established?
questions.explanation: Correct: 5. Five Regional Medical Hubs are proposed in partnership with the private sector. These Hubs will serve as integrated healthcare complexes that combine medical, educational and research facilities.

questions.question: How many new All India Institutes of Ayurveda are proposed?
questions.explanation: Correct: 3. Three new All India Institutes of Ayurveda are proposed

questions.question: By 2030, how many professionals are projected to be required in the AVGC sector?
questions.explanation: Correct: 2 million. The AVGC sector is projected to require 2 million professionals by 2030

questions.question: How many AVGC Content Creator Labs are proposed in secondary schools and colleges?
questions.explanation: Correct: 15,000 schools and 500 colleges. India's Animation, Visual Effects, Gaming and Comics (AVGC) sector is a growing industry, projected to require 2 million professionals by 2030.

questions.question: How many University Townships are proposed near industrial corridors?
questions.explanation: Correct: 5. Five University Townships are proposed in the vicinity of major industrial and logistic corridors. These planned academic zones will host multiple universities, colleges, research institutions, skill centres and residential complexes.

questions.question: How many Telescope Infrastructure facilities will be set up or upgraded for Astrophysics?
questions.explanation: Correct: 4.

questions.question: How many guides are proposed to be upskilled under the pilot scheme?
questions.explanation: Correct: 10,000. a pilot scheme for upskilling 10,000 guides in 20 iconic tourist sites through a standardized, high-quality 12-week training course in hybrid mode, in collaboration with an Indian Institute of Management.

questions.question: What percentage of farm income does livestock contribute, including poor and marginal households?
questions.explanation: Correct: 16%. Livestock contributes close to 16% of farm income, including of poor and marginal households.

questions.question: How many veterinary professionals are targeted to be scaled up?
questions.explanation: Correct: 20,000. Collaboration between Indian and foreign institutions will also be facilitated.

questions.question: To provide quality employment opportunities in rural and peri-urban areas, Government will support the Animal Husbandry Sector through various initiatives. Which of the following is NOT an initiative?

options.b: Scaling-up and modernisation of livestock enterprises
options.c: Creation of livestock, dairy and poultry-focused integrated value chains
options.d: Minimum Support Price for livestock products

questions.explanation: Correct: Minimum Support Price for livestock products. The listed initiatives focus on subsidy support, modernisation, value chains and Livestock FPOs.

questions.question: How many integrated-value chains are proposed for fisheries development?
questions.explanation: Correct: 500 reservoirs. Govt.

questions.question: How many people depend on coconuts for their livelihood?
questions.explanation: Correct: 30 million. India is the world's largest producer of coconuts. About 30 million people, including nearly 10 million farmers, depend on coconuts for their livelihood.

questions.question: By what year is the target to transform Indian Cashew and Cocoa into premium global brands?
questions.explanation: Correct: 2030.

questions.question: Bharat-VISTAAR is proposed as:

options.b: A multilingual AI tool integrating AgriStack portals
options.c: A direct benefit transfer system for farmers

questions.explanation: Correct: A multilingual AI tool integrating AgriStack portals. This will enhance farm productivity, enable better decisions for farmers and reduce risk by providing customised advisory support.

questions.question: Building on the success of the Lakhpati Didi Programme, I propose to help women take the next step from credit-led livelihoods to being owners of enterprises. SHE-Marts will be set up as:

options.c: Community-owned retail outlets within cluster level federations

questions.explanation: Correct: Community-owned retail outlets within cluster level federations. Building on the success of the Lakhpati Didi Programme, I propose to help women take the next step from credit-led livelihoods to being owners of enterprises.

questions.question: Which tree is specifically mentioned as closely linked to India's social and cultural heritage?
questions.explanation: Correct: Sandalwood. Sandalwood is closely linked to India's social and cultural heritage. Our Government will partner with State Governments to promote focused cultivation and post-harvest processing to restore the glory of the Indian Sandalwood ecosystem.

questions.question: 15 archaeological sites are proposed to be developed into experiential cultural destinations, Which of the following is NOT among the archaeological sites mentioned?
questions.explanation: Correct: Sanchi. 15 archeological sites including Lothal, Dholavira, Rakhigarhi, Adichanallur, Sarnath, Hastinapur, and Leh Palace into vibrant, experiential cultural destinations

questions.question: The Khelo India Mission aims to transform the sports sector over the next decade. Which of the following is NOT a stated facilitation objective of The Mission?

options.a: Integration of sports science and technology.
options.d: Systematic development of coaches and support staff.

questions.explanation: Correct: Privatization of all sports facilities.. The Khelo India Mission will transform the Sports sector over the next decade.

questions.question: As per budget 2026-27 announcements, Where will Mountain trails be developed?

options.a: Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand and Jammu and Kashmir

questions.explanation: Correct: Both a and b. Mountain trails in Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand and Jammu and Kashmir; Araku Valley in Eastern Ghats and Podhigai Malai in Western Ghats

questions.question: Turtle Trails will be developed along nesting sites in coastal areas of:

options.c: Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh and Odisha
options.d: West Bengal, Odisha and Andhra Pradesh

questions.explanation: Correct: Odisha, Karnataka and Kerala. Turtle Trails along key nesting sites in the coastal areas of Odisha, Karnataka and Kerala;

questions.question: Bird watching trails will be developed along Pulikat lake in:
questions.explanation: Correct: Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu. Bird watching trails along Pulikat lake in Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu

questions.question: The International Big Cat Alliance was established in:
questions.explanation: Correct: 2024. Under the visionary leadership of Honorable Prime Minister, we established the International Big Cat Alliance in 2024.

questions.question: The new Income Tax Act, 2025 will come into effect from:
questions.explanation: Correct: 1st April 2026. The Income Tax Act, 2025 will come into effect from 1st April 2026

questions.question: What is the proposed TCS rate on sale of overseas tour programme package?
questions.explanation: Correct: 2%. Reduced from current 5% and 20% to 2% without any stipulation of amount

questions.question: What is the proposed TCS rate for education and medical purposes under LRS?
questions.explanation: Correct: 2%. Reduced from 5% to 2% Also any interest awarded by the Motor Accident Claims Tribunal to a natural person will be exempt from Income Tax, and any TDS on this account will be done away with.

questions.question: The timeline for revising returns is proposed to be extended from 31st December to:
questions.explanation: Correct: 31st March. Extended to up to 31st March with nominal fee Individuals with ITR 1 and ITR 2 returns will continue to file till 31st July and non-audit business cases or trusts are proposed to be allowed time till 31st August.

questions.question: For foreign asset disclosure scheme Category A taxpayers (who did not disclose overseas income/assets), the limit is:
questions.explanation: Correct: Up to ₹1 crore. For category (A), the limit of undisclosed income/asset is proposed to be up to 1 crore rupees. For category (B), asset value is proposed to be up to 5 crore rupees.

questions.question: The prepayment amount for appeals is being reduced from 20% to:
questions.explanation: Correct: 10%. Quantum of pre-payment is being reduced from 20% to 10%.

questions.question: What is the safe harbour margin proposed for Information Technology Services?
questions.explanation: Correct: 15.5%. A common safe harbour margin of 15.5% applicable to all IT services.

questions.question: The threshold for availing safe harbour for IT services is enhanced from ₹300 crore to:
questions.explanation: Correct: ₹2,000 crore. The threshold for availing safe harbour for IT services is being enhanced substantially from 300 crore rupees to 2,000 crore rupees.

questions.question: Recognising the need to enable critical infrastructure and boost investment in data centres, I propose to provide tax holiday till 2047 to any foreign company that provides cloud services to customers globally by using data centre services from India. It will, however, need to provide services to Indian customers through an Indian reseller entity:
questions.explanation: Correct: 2047. Recognising the need to enable critical infrastructure and boost investment in data centres, I propose to provide tax holiday till 2047 to any foreign company that provides cloud services to customers globally by using data centre services from India.

questions.question: What safe harbour margin is proposed for related entities providing data centre services?
questions.explanation: Correct: 15% on cost. A safe harbour of 15 percent on cost is proposed for related entities providing data centre services.

questions.question: For non-residents providing capital goods to toll manufacturers in bonded zones, the tax exemption period is:
questions.explanation: Correct: 5 years. Exemption from income tax for 5 years is proposed.

questions.question: Non-resident experts can get exemption from global income for a stay period of:
questions.explanation: Correct: 5 years. To encourage vast pool of global talent to work in India for a longer period of time, government proposed to provide exemption to global (non-India sourced) income of a non-resident expert, for a stay period of 5 years under notified schemes.

questions.question: The MAT rate is being reduced from 15% to:
questions.explanation: Correct: 14%. The rate of final tax is being reduced to 14 percent from the current MAT rate of 15 percent. Government proposed to provide exemption from Minimum Alternate Tax (MAT) to all non-residents who pay tax on presumptive basis.

questions.question: The effective tax rate for corporate promoters in buyback will be:
questions.explanation: Correct: 22%. the effective rate on gains in buyback will be 22% for promoters which are domestic companies and 30% for promoters other than domestic companies.

questions.question: STT on Futures is proposed to be raised from 0.02% to:
questions.explanation: Correct: 0.05%. STT on Futures is proposed to be raised to 0.05% from present 0.02%.

questions.question: STT on options premium is proposed to be raised from 0.1% to:
questions.explanation: Correct: 0.15%. STT on options premium is proposed to be raised to 0.15% from present 0.1%

questions.question: Which of the following TCS rates is being rationalized to 2%?

options.d: All of the above / all listed items

questions.explanation: Correct: All listed TCS categories. TCS on alcoholic liquor, scrap, minerals and tendu leaves is rationalized/reduced to 2%.

questions.question: The basic customs duty exemption for Nuclear Power Projects is extended till:
questions.explanation: Correct: 2035. The existing basic customs duty exemption is extended till 2035

questions.question: The tariff rate on all dutiable goods imported for personal use is reduced from 20% to:
questions.explanation: Correct: 10%. The tariff rate on all dutiable goods imported for personal use is reduced from 20 per cent to 10 per cent.

questions.question: The value limit for duty-free imports of inputs for seafood processing is increased from 1% to:
questions.explanation: Correct: 3%. Increased from current 1 per cent to 3 per cent of FOB value of previous year's export.

questions.question: The time period for export of final products for leather/textile exporters is extended from 6 months to:
questions.explanation: Correct: 12 months. Extended from existing 6 months to 1 year

questions.question: For Authorised Economic Operators Tier 2 and Tier 3, the duty deferral period is enhanced from 15 days to:
questions.explanation: Correct: 30 days. Government has proposed to enhance duty deferral period for Tier 2 and Tier 3 Authorised Economic Operators, known as AEOs, from 15 days to 30 days.

questions.question: The validity period of advance ruling is extended from 3 years to:
questions.explanation: Correct: 5 years. Extended from present 3 years to 5 years

questions.question: The current value cap of ₹10 lakh per consignment on courier exports is:
questions.explanation: Correct: Completely removed. Complete removal of the current value cap of ₹10 lakh per consignment

questions.question: How many new drugs/medicines are proposed to be exempted from basic customs duty?
questions.explanation: Correct: 17. Exemption of basic customs duty on 17 drugs or medicines.

questions.question: National Calamity Contingent Duty (NCCD) Rate with effect from 01.05.2026 on Chewing tobacco products will be:
questions.explanation: Correct: 60%. National Calamity Contingent Duty (NCCD) is a specific excise duty imposed on certain goods (notably tobacco and petroleum products).

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, which of the following correctly states the revised deduction period and post-deduction tax rate for units in an International Financial Services Centre (IFSC)?

options.a: 10 consecutive years out of 15 years; 22% tax thereafter
options.b: 15 consecutive years out of 20 years; 20% tax thereafter
options.c: 20 consecutive years out of 25 years; 15% tax thereafter
options.d: 20 consecutive years out of 25 years; 22% tax thereafter

questions.explanation: Correct: 20 consecutive years out of 25 years; 15% tax thereafter. IFSC units get deduction for 20 consecutive years out of 25 years, and post-deduction business income is taxed at 15%.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, India's economic trajectory over the last 12 years has been marked by:

options.c: Stability, fiscal discipline, sustained growth and moderate inflation
options.d: Rapid growth led mainly by private consumption

questions.explanation: Correct: Stability, fiscal discipline, sustained growth and moderate inflation. In 12 years, India's economic trajectory has been marked by stability, fiscal discipline, sustained growth and moderate inflation.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, the Government has simultaneously pursued which of the following policy combinations?

options.c: Structural reforms, fiscal prudence and monetary stability

questions.explanation: Correct: Structural reforms, fiscal prudence and monetary stability. Government have pursued far reaching structural reforms, fiscal prudence and monetary stability whilst maintaining a strong thrust on public investment.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, the threefold approach that requires a supportive ecosystem consists of which of the following?

options.a: Fiscal expansion, monetary easing and welfare transfers
options.b: Structural reforms, export promotion and subsidy rationalisation
options.c: to sustain the momentum of structural reforms, a robust and resilient financial sector, and cutting-edge technologies (including AI)
options.d: Public investment, private participation and global integration

questions.explanation: Correct: to sustain the momentum of structural reforms, a robust and resilient financial sector, and cutting-edge technologies (including AI). This threefold approach requires a supportive ecosystem. The first requirement is to sustain the momentum of structural reforms-continuous, adaptive, and forward-looking.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, approximately how many reforms have been rolled out following the 2025 announcement?
questions.explanation: Correct: Over 350. Government has undertaken comprehensive economic reforms towards creating employment, boosting productivity and accelerating growth.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, We shall continue to focus on developing infrastructure in cities with over _____ population (Tier II and Tier III), which have expanded to become growth centres.
questions.explanation: Correct: 5 lakh. We shall continue to focus on developing infrastructure in cities with over 5 lakh population (Tier II and Tier III), which have expanded to become growth centres.

questions.question: As per Budget Speech 2026-27, Close to _____ individuals have come out of multidimensional poverty through a decade of our Government's sustained and reform-oriented efforts.
questions.explanation: Correct: 25 crore. Our second kartavya is to fulfil aspirations and build capacity. Close to 25 crore individuals have come out of multidimensional poverty through a decade of our Government's sustained and reform-oriented efforts

questions.question: The FRBM Act, 2003, and the corresponding FRBM Rules came into force on:
questions.explanation: Correct: 5th July 2004. The Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management Act, 2003 and the Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management Rules, 2004 made under Section 8 of the Act came into force from 5th July 2004.

questions.question: As per the FRBM framework, what are the debt-to-GDP ratio targets that the Central Government shall endeavour to achieve by 31st March, 2025?

options.a: General Government Debt: 50% of GDP; Central Government Debt: 30% of GDP
options.b: General Government Debt: 55% of GDP; Central Government Debt: 35% of GDP
options.c: General Government Debt: 60% of GDP; Central Government Debt: 40% of GDP
options.d: General Government Debt: 65% of GDP; Central Government Debt: 45% of GDP

questions.explanation: Correct: General Government Debt: 60% of GDP; Central Government Debt: 40% of GDP. The FRBM framework mandates the Central Government to limit the Fiscal Deficit upto three per cent of Gross Domestic Product by 31st March, 2021.

questions.question: As per Budget 2026-27, the revenue deficit (BE 2026-27) is estimated at:
questions.explanation: Correct: 1.5%. The revenue deficit for BE 2026-27 is estimated at 1.5% of GDP.

questions.question: As per Budget 2026-27, In BE 2026-27, the GTR to GDP ratio is estimated at:
questions.explanation: Correct: 11.2%. In BE 2026-27, Gross Tax Revenue (GTR) is estimated at ₹44.04 lakh crore. It represents a growth of 8.0 per cent over RE 2025-26.
